comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report


Last Updated: 3/13/26 | 1:08PM

Good afternoon, Sunday River!

We are currently in the "calm before the storm" part of the day. The sun is out, the groomers are skiing incredibly well, and temps are fairly moderate, sitting in the 20s.

Tonight will look a little different. Mother Nature is shaking the snow globe again. Once the sun goes down, the snow will start falling. The bulk of the storm will come overnight, but we should see an inch or two of refreshers throughout the day tomorrow to keep things fresh and make Saturday even better. All in all, weather experts are calling for 3 to 6 inches by the end of the day tomorrow.

If the snow isn't enough reason for you to be here, we also have a stacked lineup of fun scheduled, including a Bissell Brothers takeover at the Igloo with DJ NDB throwing down some hot beats.

The time has come to renew your New England Pass for the 2026/27 season. If you don't have one this season, you can still get one at the lowest price of the year. As an added bonus, you can ski the rest of this season as well. Either way, now is the time to buy if you want to get the most bang for your buck.

And lastly, for passholders, this weekend we're dropping some great deals at our retail shops. Grab up to 60% off at all retail locations, including Sunday River Sports, the Kuhl Store at the Summit, Aster & Birch, The Jordan Gift Shop, and Betty's Treats & Treasures.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good morning, Sunday River.

Right on cue, Mother Nature would like to remind everyone that winter is not done yet. Yesterday, temps took a nosedive, dropping about 30 to 40 degrees in a hurry. Classic March in New England. One minute you're thinking about grilling, the next you're digging the big jacket back out of the closet.

It's a beautiful start to the day with a pretty unreal sunrise lighting up the 8 peaks this morning.

Conditions are skiing great out there. Soft snow that lets you really dig in an edge and let it run. After a few nights of dialing back the grooming plan, the crew fired things back up last night and laid down fresh cord across the mountain, so plenty of trails are looking sharp for those early laps.

Temperature-wise, we're sitting at 12 degrees at Locke Peak and 22 down at the White Cap base. It's a bit windy this morning with northwest winds around 20 to 25 mph and gusts up to 45. The good news is those winds should ease up as the day goes on, settling closer to 10 to 15 mph this afternoon. Temps should slowly climb as well, working their way into the 20s across the mountain later today while clouds gradually build in.

Now onto the good stuff. Tomorrow morning is bringing some fresh snow. Forecasts are a little split right now. Some say 2 to 4 inches, others are calling for 4 to 8. Naturally, we're pulling for the bigger number. Either way, more snow is on the way, so it might be worth setting that early alarm tonight.

And last but not least, the 2026/27 New England Pass is officially on sale. If you're a current passholder, you can lock in the renewal rate. If you didn't have one this season, you can still grab one at the lowest price of the year, and buying now lets you ski the rest of this season too. Either way, now's the time to get the most bang for your buck.

Winter's back. Come enjoy it with us.

-Team Snow
